tweet: But, the rules of the House limit virtually all action and debate to the majority – in this case, led by Speaker Pelosi. Because the damage to the institution would be so significant and immediate, it demands we resolve this question quickly to protect the Constitution and true..

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: America is flexible. We certainly can adapt and use technology where appropriate to streamline hearings, committee work, and share information. The Constitution rightfully contemplates a quorum be present, and that no member of Congress passes off his or her obligation to vote...

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: ...while another member his or her vote and put to ten additional votes.  For this reason, I am proud that a constituent of mine has also joined the litigation to preserve proper representation.

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: Our constituents delegate to their elected member, and their elected member alone, the duty to decide votes on their behalf. Perhaps more – this new rule would dilute the voting representation of Americans whose representative casts, properly, only his or her vote...

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: The American People send their representatives to Washington to represent them, which in no small part means casting votes for them. By adopting the new rule, they are allowing Representatives to delegate away something that is un-delegable.

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: so-called “proxy” – to another member. We did not allow proxy voting while the nation was engulfed in the Civil War. We didn’t allow it while the Capitol building was on fire during the War of 1812. We didn’t allow it through World Wars and the Spanish Flu. We didn’t after 9/11.

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: As I’ve said, reasonable people can disagree about the specifics of securing our border, the appropriate levels of immigration, and other specifics. No one can disagree that these families should not have lost a loved one because we refuse to get anything done.

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: What Americans see on a day-to-day basis is what is happening North of our border, or in English-speaking media. Americans aren’t the only ones suffering from our failures. Lives are being taken North and South of our border. Mexican drug cartels have operational control.

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: Jared, Spencer, Tessa, and Allison aren’t alone. 41-year-old Purple Heart and Medal of Valor winner Don Johnston was shot by an illegal immigrant. Johnston, a police officer, was answering a call at a bank.

tweet: Prior to killing Tessa and Allison, Ramos ran a cop off the road while drunk. Officers ticketed, sentenced, and fined him. Yet, the local sheriff released him. Tessa’s mother claims @ICEgov was never contacted.

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: Alfredo Ramos--the immigrant who killed Tessa and Allison--purchased his fake license from a cartel. As was the case with Jared and Spencer’s respective killers, Ramos was previously arrested.

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office

tweet: BP agents have shown me videos of cartels beating migrants. They are beating them to within an inch of their lives. They then send the videos to the migrant's family. Why? Extortion.   We've seen estimates that cartels and traffickers are making $2 bill running human beings.

tweet: Cartels are bending people to their will.   @CBP reported dashing three stash houses last month, resulting in the discovery of 26 migrants.   Cartels are often running migrants in and out of these stash houses as they await payment. These migrants are held and sometimes beaten.

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: We are in danger of losing what separates us from the other nations of the world because we have allowed our borders to descend into chaos and lawlessness while sanctuary cities thwart federal law. (7/x)

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: Jared’s murderer had been in ICE custody twice and had been arrested for a DWI and released just a month before he killed Jared. (6/x)

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: The government failed the Vargas family because instead of securing our nation’s border and enforcing the law, members on both sides of the aisle have spent the last few decades arguing about policy instead of taking action. (5/x)

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: Jared’s life was cut tragically short when he was brutally murdered by an illegal alien in San Antonio last June. The current debate and immigration crisis before us has a face, it has a name. (4/x)

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: The current debate about immigration policies and the crisis along our southern border isn’t just about facts or statistics, it’s about people like Jared Vargas – a vibrant young college student studying computer science with dreams of working in the cyber security field. (3/x)

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: It has been a tremendous blessing to get to know the Vargas family and our thoughts and prayers are with them today. (2/x)

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: Carrah and I rejoice with the Vargas family that justice for Jared was finally served when his murderer was found guilty in San Antonio today. But as parents, our hearts are heavy because no parent should endure what Jared’s parents and family have suffered. (1/x)

name: Rep. Chip Roy Press Office
tweet: Those who attempt to thwart the rule of law and subvert our political processes through violence should never be allowed to succeed in their objectives.  ICE & CBP should not be deterred in their efforts to secure the border or enforce the immigration laws of this country. (6/6)

tweet: We pray for the safety of all our law enforcement officials, for the swift prosecution of the perpetrators, and for common sense and civil society to prevail. (5/x)

tweet: Any incitement of violence against our law enforcement is unacceptable and has no place in San Antonio or anywhere else in this nation. (4/x)
